stem: A precision manufacturing study measures bolt diameter for 20 samples to determine if the process variability is within ±0.05mm of the target. The sample standard deviation is 3.74 mm (sample variance = 13.96). Estimate the true population standard deviation of bolt diameter and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Sample SD = 3.74 mm. 95% CI for σ (chi-squared): [2.84, 5.46]. Point estimate: 3.74 mm.

common_failure_modes: Using z-based CI for the mean instead of χ²-based CI for the variance
Reporting the sample SD (3.74) as the population SD without uncertainty
Not recognizing that confidence intervals for variance are asymmetric
